title: convolvulus waitaha
intro: Convolvulus waitaha, also known as the Waitaha vine, is a plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the bignonia family and characterized by its woody stems and fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Convolvulus waitaha include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the leaves and flowers being the primary components used for medicinal purposes.
